substep.title: Compose per-Category Scan Questions
substep.action:
For each selected category, compose the scan questions that probe the approach for that category's failure modes, routed per references/research/child-subagent-dispatch.md: - Our exposure: "does our code already exhibit {category risk pattern} where this approach touches it?" → codebase-analyzer / codebase-pattern-finder - Prior incidents: "have we hit {category} problems like this before?" → thoughts-locator (then thoughts-analyzer) - Known external risks: "what are the known {category} risks / CVEs / failure modes for {technique or dependency}?" → web-search-researcher
Frame each scan adversarially — ask what breaks, not how it works.

check.if: any scan routes to web-search-researcher AND INTERACTIVE
check.ask: Name the scans needing external/web research (known CVEs, benchmarks, compliance requirements). Ask the user to approve dispatching web-search-researcher, recommended option first. If declined, mark those categories as assessed from internal evidence only and note the limitation.

check.if: any scan routes to web-search-researcher AND AUTONOMOUS
check.action:
If web_search_approved is true, keep the external scans. If false or absent, emit to stdout and stop:
CLARIFICATION_NEEDED: Risk scan needs web research for {category/categories}, but web_search_approved was not set. Re-dispatch with web_search_approved=true, or limit to internal-evidence assessment.

substep.title: Dispatch and Validate
substep.action: Dispatch independent scans in parallel waves via the Task tool (the two-stage thoughts pattern runs locator then analyzer). Each prompt carries the scan question alone, never the parent task's intent. Redispatch any vague return with a sharper prompt. A "no instances found" return for a scanned risk is a genuine finding — it lowers that risk's likelihood; keep it.
